text: An apology is the superglue of life! It can repair just about anything.
author: Lynn Johnston

text: Never ruin an apology with an excuse.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: The first to apologize is the bravest. The first to forgive is the strongest. The first to forget is the happiest.
author: Unknown

text: A genuine apology is like an antibiotic; a fake apology is like a sugar pill.
author: Randy Pausch

text: Apologizing does not always mean you are wrong and the other person is right. It means you value your relationship more than your ego.
author: Unknown

text: Forgiveness does not change the past, but it does enlarge the future.
author: Paul Boese

text: The weak can never forgive. Forgiveness is the attribute of the strong.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: To err is human; to forgive, divine.
author: Alexander Pope

text: Mistakes are always forgivable, if one has the courage to admit them.
author: Bruce Lee

text: An apology is a good way to have the last word.
author: Unknown

text: Forgiveness is the fragrance that the violet sheds on the heel that has crushed it.
author: Mark Twain

text: It takes a great deal of character strength to apologize quickly out of one's heart rather than out of pity.
author: Stephen Covey

text: Chocolate says 'I'm sorry' so much better than words.
author: Rachel Vincent

text: True remorse is never just a regret over consequence; it is a regret over motive.
author: Mignon McLaughlin

text: Would 'sorry' have made any difference? Does it ever? It's just a word. One word against a thousand actions.
author: Sarah Ockler

text: Right actions in the future are the best apologies for bad actions in the past.
author: Tryon Edwards

text: An apology given just to appease one's conscience is self-serving and better left unspoken.
author: Evinda Lepins

text: Forgiveness is not an occasional act; it is a constant attitude.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: Sorry means you feel the pulse of other people's pain as well as your own, and saying it means you take a share of it.
author: Craig Silvey

text: Apology is a lovely perfume; it can transform the clumsiest moment into a gracious gift.
author: Margaret Lee Runbeck

text: When you forgive, you in no way change the past — but you sure do change the future.
author: Bernard Meltzer

text: Forgive others, not because they deserve forgiveness, but because you deserve peace.
author: Jonathan Lockwood Huie

text: The art of a sincere and heartfelt apology is one of the greatest skills you will ever learn.
author: Jeanette LeBlanc

text: He who is devoid of the power to forgive is devoid of the power to love.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: Life becomes easier when you learn to accept an apology you never got.
author: Robert Brault

text: A stiff apology is a second insult. The injured party does not want to be compensated because he has been wronged; he wants to be healed because he has been hurt.
author: G.K. Chesterton

text: Forgiveness is a gift you give yourself.
author: Tony Robbins

text: Never forget the nine most important words of any family: I love you. You are beautiful. Please forgive me.
author: H. Jackson Brown Jr.

text: In some families, 'please' is described as the magic word. In our house, however, it was 'sorry.'
author: Margaret Laurence

text: Apologies aren't meant to change the past, they are meant to change the future.
author: Kevin Hancock

text: Genuine apology means you have the courage to be humble.
author: Steve Brunkhorst

text: Saying sorry to someone is hard, but putting your pride aside for someone is even harder.
author: Unknown

text: An apology might be classified as the acid test of character.
author: Unknown

text: The bitterest tears shed over graves are for words left unsaid and deeds left undone.
author: Harriet Beecher Stowe

text: Forgiveness is the final form of love.
author: Reinhold Niebuhr

text: I am sorry for the pain I caused you. If I could take it back, I would.
author: Unknown

text: We must develop and maintain the capacity to forgive. He who is devoid of the power to forgive is devoid of the power to love.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: It is easier to forgive an enemy than to forgive a friend.
author: William Blake

text: Forgiveness says you are given another chance to make a new beginning.
author: Desmond Tutu

text: Without forgiveness, there's no future.
author: Desmond Tutu

text: My bad deeds do not define me. My response to them does.
author: Unknown

text: An apology without change is just manipulation.
author: Unknown

text: The best apology is changed behavior.
author: Unknown

text: I was wrong. I'm sorry. Three little words that can mend almost anything, when they are truly meant.
author: Unknown

text: Humility is the true key to an honest apology.
author: Unknown

text: Anyone can make mistakes, but only the wise learn from them and seek forgiveness.
author: Unknown

text: Forgive me for my mistakes, I am still a child learning the lessons of life.
author: Unknown

text: Sometimes 'sorry' is not enough, but it is always a place to start.
author: Unknown

text: Real regret is shown through actions, not just spoken words.
author: Unknown

text: To forgive is to set a prisoner free and discover that the prisoner was you.
author: Lewis B. Smedes

text: The truth is, unless you let go, unless you forgive yourself, unless you forgive the situation, unless you realize that the situation is over, you cannot move forward.
author: Steve Maraboli

text: When a deep injury is done to us, we never heal until we forgive.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: Forgiveness liberates the soul. It removes fear. That is why it is such a powerful weapon.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: I can have peace of mind only when I forgive rather than judge.
author: Gerald Jampolsky

text: Forgiveness is choosing to love. It is the first skill of self-giving love.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: There is no love without forgiveness, and there is no forgiveness without love.
author: Bryant H. McGill

text: Forgiveness is me giving up the right to hurt you for hurting me.
author: Anonymous

text: Only the brave know how to forgive. A coward never forgives; it is not in his nature.
author: Laurence Sterne

text: Let us forgive each other — only then will we live in peace.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: Forgiveness is above all a personal choice, a decision of the heart to go against the natural instinct to pay back evil with evil.
author: Pope John Paul II

text: Nothing inspires forgiveness quite like revenge.
author: Scott Adams

text: Before we can forgive one another, we have to understand one another.
author: Emma Goldman

text: How unhappy is he who cannot forgive himself.
author: Publilius Syrus

text: It's one thing to say you're sorry. It's another thing entirely to show it.
author: Unknown

text: Apologies require vulnerability, and vulnerability requires courage.
author: Unknown

text: If I did anything wrong, it was loving you too much and showing it too little.
author: Unknown

text: An honest apology is a bridge back to the ones we hurt.
author: Unknown

text: When you realize you've made a mistake, make amends immediately. It's easier to eat crow while it's still warm.
author: Dan Heist

text: Keep your words soft and tender because tomorrow you may have to eat them.
author: Unknown

text: Apology accepted does not always mean trust restored. Give it time.
author: Unknown

text: I'm sorry isn't always enough to repair the damage, but it's the only honest place to begin.
author: Unknown

text: Pride makes apologies hard; love makes them possible.
author: Unknown

text: The hardest words to say are often the most important ones: I was wrong, and I am sorry.
author: Unknown

text: A meaningful apology has three parts: I'm sorry. It was my fault. How do I make it right?
author: Unknown

text: Own your mistakes before they own you.
author: Unknown

text: Being sorry is the first step. Being better is the rest of the journey.
author: Unknown

text: Sincere apologies are for those that make them, not for those to whom they are made.
author: Greg LeMond

text: Never apologize for showing feeling. When you do so, you apologize for the truth.
author: Benjamin Disraeli

text: An apology is the first step toward healing a wounded relationship.
author: Unknown

text: Forgiving does not erase the bitter past. A healed memory is not a deleted memory. Instead, forgiving what we cannot forget creates a new way to remember.
author: Lewis B. Smedes

text: We are all mistaken sometimes; sometimes we do wrong things, things that have bad consequences. But it does not mean we are evil.
author: Alison Croggon

text: One of the most lasting pleasures you can experience is the feeling that comes over you when you genuinely forgive an enemy — whether he knows it or not.
author: O.A. Battista

text: To forgive is wisdom, to forget is genius.
author: Joyce Cary

text: Gorgeous hair is the best revenge — but a sincere apology is the best resolution.
author: Unknown

text: Love in the real world means saying you're sorry ten times a day.
author: Kathie Lee Gifford

text: A relationship that survives an honest apology grows stronger at the broken places.
author: Unknown

text: Sometimes the person you hurt the most is the person you love the most.
author: Unknown

text: I would rather lose my pride to keep you than keep my pride and lose you.
author: Unknown

text: Words spoken in anger are the hardest to take back, and the most important to apologize for.
author: Unknown

text: Forgiveness is a funny thing. It warms the heart and cools the sting.
author: William Arthur Ward

text: Forgiveness means letting go of the hope for a better past.
author: Lama Surya Das

text: The remedy for wrongs is to forget them.
author: Publilius Syrus

text: Anger makes you smaller, while forgiveness forces you to grow beyond what you were.
author: Cherie Carter-Scott

text: There is nothing so advantageous to a man than a forgiving disposition.
author: Terence

text: If we all hold on to the mistake, we can't see our own glory in the mirror because we have the mistake between our faces and the mirror.
author: Maya Angelou

text: You will know that forgiveness has begun when you recall those who hurt you and feel the power to wish them well.
author: Lewis B. Smedes

text: The past cannot be changed, but a sincere apology can change everything that comes after it.
author: Unknown

text: I know saying sorry won't undo what happened, but I hope it can be the start of making things right.
author: Unknown

text: Every saint has a past, and every sinner has a future.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: Forgiveness is the key that unlocks the door of resentment and the handcuffs of hatred.
author: Corrie ten Boom
